description: Supersede or invalidate selected memory through an auditable correction.
name: correct

description: Forget by memory unit, episode or resource selector; tombstones block re-derivation.
name: forget

description: Report what the caller did with a recall pack (feeds decay/reinforcement).
name: mark

description: Retrieve cited memory evidence for a query (budgeted, salience-ranked, with provenance).

from.description: Inclusive RFC 3339 lower bound supplied by the host.

to.description: Exclusive RFC 3339 upper bound supplied by the host.

name: recall

consolidation_lag_ms.description:
Non-zero when `degraded: true`: recall drew on raw un-reflected
episodes because consolidation had not caught up (spec 08 §4).

description: Consolidate a scope's pending episodes/resources into memory units.
name: reflect

description: Store exactly one episode, resource, or direct unit with provenance.

RetainEpisodeHttpRequest.description: The retain verb request with exactly one tagged episode, resource, or unit payload.

RetainResourcePayload.description: Resource payload for the retain verb (spec 08 §209 `resource` shape).

body.description: Durable resource content the worker compiles from.

revision.description: Revision identity (e.g. a code commit hash).

RetainUnitPayload.description:
Direct pre-compiled unit payload for trusted callers (spec 08 §209 `unit`
shape). Requires an explicit fact key, predicate, confidence, and kind; the admission
trust policy still applies.

name: retain

assigned_trust.description:
The trust tier actually assigned after clamping to the API key's
`max_trust` ceiling.

cross_rerank_ms.description:
R1.5-T1: per-recall wall-clock (ms) spent inside the W8 cross-encoder
rerank stage ([`crate::CrossReranker`], distinct from the retired
heuristic `reranker_id`/`rerank_input_count`/`rerank_overfetch_ratio`
fields below). `0` when no cross-reranker is installed on the service
(the default) or the candidate pool was empty — a legitimate "not
run" value, not a sentinel. `#[serde(default)]` so traces recorded
before this field existed still deserialize.

recall_pool_depth.description:
R1.5-T0: the `recall_pool_depth` in effect for this recall — the ONE
knob the vector/rerank/packing-scan internal fan-out derived from
(never from `k`). Recorded per-trace so latency attribution stays
observable across deployments that override
`MEMPHANT_RECALL_POOL_DEPTH`. `#[serde(default)]` so traces recorded
before this field existed still deserialize (as `0`, a visible "not
recorded" sentinel — pool depth is never legitimately 0).
